title: Local Persistence of Lagrangian Intersections
speaker: Rémi Leclercq
abstract: Given a Lagrangian L, I will discuss the existence of a neighborhood W of L with the following property: for any Hamiltonian diffeomorphism f, if f(L) is contained inside W, then f(L) intersects L. On the one hand, for any symplectic manifold of dimension at least 6, I will construct Lagrangians which do not admit any such neighborhoods. On the other hand, I will give conditions which ensure the existence of such neighborhoods for a large class of Lagrangians. These conditions actually ensure the exactness of certain nearby Lagrangians, and I will discuss further applications of this phenomenon. This is based on joint work with Marcelo Atallah, Jean-Philippe Chassé, and Egor Shelukhin.

proof_stages.label: Local persistence and path-connectedness questions
proof_stages.summary: The speaker fixes a closed connected Lagrangian L, defines its Hamiltonian, symplectic, and Lagrangian-isotopy orbits, asks for local path-connectedness in these orbits, and extracts the weaker question of local persistence of intersections using a Weinstein neighborhood and exact Lagrangian intersection results in T^*L.

proof_stages.label: Motivating dichotomy and period data
proof_stages.summary: Simple low-dimensional models show that Hamiltonian and symplectic persistence can behave differently, that local phenomena can be implanted in small balls, and that the H_1 map induced by L included in M and relative period groups must enter the hypotheses.

proof_stages.label: Main theorem package and hypotheses
proof_stages.summary: The talk states the negative theorem for irrational Lagrangian tori with arbitrarily close disjoint Hamiltonian images and nonclosed Hamiltonian orbit, then introduces h-rationality, h-exactness, condition C, and the positive persistence and path-connectedness theorems, including their fixed-rationality variants.

proof_stages.label: Reduction to local exactness
proof_stages.summary: The positive persistence theorem is reduced to proving that the relevant nearby Lagrangians become exact in a Weinstein chart, via two steps: nearby fixed-rationality Lagrangians are h-exact in a small neighborhood, and h-exactness in the ambient neighborhood implies exactness in T^*L for Hamiltonian images or under the H_1-vanishing condition.

proof_stages.label: Irrational split-torus counterexamples
proof_stages.summary: The negative theorem is proved from Chekanov's classification of Hamiltonian isotopy classes of split tori in C^3, density of the integer span generated by an irrational parameter, convergence both to nearby disjoint Hamiltonian images and to a non-orbit limit, and a bounded-ball realization that embeds the construction into any symplectic manifold of dimension at least six.

proof_stages.label: Capacity proof of h-exactness neighborhoods
proof_stages.summary: The first exactness theorem is proved using Chekanov–Mohnke-type min-max capacities, their scaling and partial monotonicity properties, finiteness supplied by a Liouville-domain embedding with vanishing symplectic homology, and the resulting Lipschitz control of h-rationality constants as Weinstein neighborhoods shrink.

proof_stages.label: From h-exactness to exactness
proof_stages.summary: The second exactness theorem uses Polterovich-style perturbation and graph-selector estimates to bound Liouville periods by the neighborhood size, then combines this with the discreteness of periods coming from fixed h-rationality and the Hamiltonian or H_1-vanishing hypotheses to force all periods to vanish.

proof_stages.label: Applications to flux, orbit topology, and C^0 rigidity
proof_stages.summary: The theorem package is applied to Lagrangian C^1 and C^0 flux-type closedness results, openness and connected components of fixed h-rationality Lagrangian spaces, equality of Hamiltonian and symplectic orbits under H_1-vanishing, and a positive C^0 lower bound for Hamiltonian diffeomorphisms displacing an h-rational Lagrangian.
